items.title: Amazon Spring Sale Deal: The Typhur Dome 2 Air Fryer Is 30% Off
items.summary: I tested more than 30 air fryers this past year. The Typhur Dome 2 is the one I recommend, and it’s uncommonly cheap right now.

items.title: There’s Something Very Dark About a Lot of Those Viral AI Fruit Videos
items.summary: With female AI fruit being fart-shamed and even sexually assaulted, there’s a misogynistic undercurrent to the fruit slop microdramas, even as they appear to be cultivating genuine fans.

items.title: OpenClaw Agents Can Be Guilt-Tripped Into Self-Sabotage
items.summary: In a controlled experiment, OpenClaw agents proved prone to panic and vulnerable to manipulation. They even disabled their own functionality when gaslit by humans.

items.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
items.summary: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

items.title: Convicted spyware chief hints that Greece’s government was behind dozens of phone hacks
items.summary: The spyware founder's comments are the most direct suggestion yet from anyone inside Intellexa that the Mitsotakis government authorized the hacking of dozens of phones belonging to senior Greek government ministers, opposition leaders, military officials, and journalists.

items.title: Who’s driving Waymo’s self-driving cars? Sometimes, the police.
items.summary: First responders have had to take control of Waymo vehicles and move them during emergency situations, including in at least two active crime scenes, TechCrunch found.

items.title: Meta and YouTube found liable in landmark social media addiction trial
items.summary: A woman has been awarded $3m in a verdict that could have implications for hundreds of other cases in the US.

items.title: UN votes to recognise enslavement of Africans as 'gravest crime against humanity'
items.summary: The landmark resolution calls for an apology and contributions to a reparations fund, without specifying an amount.

items.title: Democrats flip Florida state seat that includes Trump's Mar-a-Lago home
items.summary: Emily Gregory’s projected victory comes in a legislative district which a Republican candidate won by 19 percentage points in 2024.

items.title: The research firm whose AI paper knocked the whole stock market is out with another big call
items.summary: Citrini Research said persistently high energy prices risk weighing on consumers and corporate earnings, creating a tough backdrop for equities.

items.title: Former SEC Chair Jay Clayton says regulators would scrutinize trading ahead of Trump post
items.summary: "Any move like that in advance of any announcement, the regulators are going to look at," said Clayton, former SEC chair.

items.title: World has 'never experienced' soaring refining margins like this, TotalEnergies CEO tells CNBC
items.summary: TotalEnergies CEO Patrick Pouyanné on the Iran war, the $1 billion deal with the White House, investment in the U.S., and more.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Conservatives shoot down Senate off-ramp on SAVE America Act
items.summary: Conservatives in both chambers are sharply shooting down the idea of trying to advance key provisions of the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) America Act through the budget reconciliation process, arguing the strategy has little chance of succeeding. Senate Republicans have been searching for an off-ramp to end the intense pressure campaign from President Trump…

items.title: Watch live: Trump addresses House Republicans at annual NRCC dinner
items.summary: President Trump on Wednesday evening will address House Republicans, supporters and donors at the annual National Republican Congressional Committee fundraising dinner. The event, held at Union Station in Washington, will give Trump another chance to sell his agenda ahead of the 2026 midterm elections. It also comes as GOP lawmakers face calls to find common…

items.title: Keir Starmer allows UK military to board Russia’s shadow fleet vessels
items.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will now allow his country’s military to board Russian “shadow fleet” ships, according to a Wednesday press release from his office. Starmer said the interdiction of the Russian shadow ships, part of a network of tankers with obscure ownership or insurance to evade sanctions, follows the Royal Navy’s increased support…

items.title: Why We Think Apple Is Headed to $303 Over the Next 12 Months

items.title: Metformin’s hidden brain pathway revealed after 60 years
items.summary: A major discovery reveals that metformin works not just in the body, but in the brain. By switching off a key protein and activating specific neurons, the drug lowers blood sugar through a previously hidden pathway, opening new doors for diabetes treatment.

items.title: Astronomers solve 50-year mystery of a naked-eye star’s extreme X-rays
items.summary: A star you can see with the naked eye has kept astronomers guessing for decades with its unusually powerful X-rays. Now, thanks to highly precise observations from Japan’s XRISM space telescope, scientists have finally uncovered the source: a hidden white dwarf companion pulling in material and generating extreme heat. This discovery not only solves a 50-year-old mystery surrounding Gamma Cassiopeiae, but also confirms the existence of a long-predicted type of binary star system.

items.title: Project Hail Mary meets reality: 45 planets could harbor alien life
items.summary: Astronomers have narrowed down the cosmic search for life, identifying fewer than 50 rocky planets among thousands of known exoplanets that may have the right conditions to support life. Using new data from ESA’s Gaia mission and NASA archives, researchers pinpointed worlds in the “habitable zone,” where temperatures could allow liquid water to exist. Some of the most intriguing targets include nearby systems like TRAPPIST-1 and Proxima Centauri, offering tantalizing possibilities just dozens of light-years away.

items.title: NASA races to have the first moon base and nuclear-propulsion spacecraft
items.summary: A $20 billion plan for a moon base by 2030 and the launch nuclear-propulsion space exploration raises hopes, but caution given deep government cuts.

items.title: When were dogs domesticated? The oldest known dog DNA offers clues
items.summary: Two new studies suggest that genetically stable dogs were living among humans in Europe by about 14,000 years ago.

items.title: A private moon lander challenges ideas about lunar volcanism
items.summary: New measurements from the Blue Ghost lander suggest that thin crust, not just radioactive heating, shaped the moon’s dark lava plains.
